title: Project Metadata, Resources, PyInstaller, and Platform Builds · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent accessibility club receives a different grid, seed, algorithm, input profile, display scale, assistance need, platform, and failure condition and transfers project metadata, resources, pyinstaller, and platform builds into a changed platform-native signed-off release artifact matrix. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of platform-native signed-off release artifact matrix with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Define package layout, metadata, Python requirement, dependencies, console or GUI entry point, and import-safe startup.
Load licensed package resources without assuming source checkout or current working directory layout.
Inspect PyInstaller analysis, tkinter hooks, Tcl/Tk libraries and data, hidden imports, warnings, and artifact contents.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Pyproject Entrypoint
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab puzzle team handles case 3246 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile package metadata, resources, Tcl and Tk assets, native artifacts, and clean-install launches; all graph claims need an independent witness; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Define package layout, metadata, Python requirement, dependencies, console or GUI entry point, and import-safe startup.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Clean Install Smoke
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-clean-install-smoke-d5qulb" that uses case 5150 to reconcile package metadata, resources, Tcl and Tk assets, native artifacts, and clean-install launches. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Clean Install Smoke.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Clean Install Smoke into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Clean Install Smoke, keep Clean Install Smoke connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-clean-install-smoke-d5qulb
# Competency: maze-clean-install-smoke.
# Case 5150: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_clean_install_smoke_d5qulb(target_platforms: int = 3, native_builds: int = 3, clean_launches: int = 3, missing_tk_assets: int = 0, artifact_digests: int = 3) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_d5qulb = "d5qulb-bluq5d-5150"
    assert evidence_variant_d5qulb
    evidence_axes_d5qulb = (
        "gridd5qulb",
        "topology5150",
        "searchbluq5d",
        "eventd5qulb5150",
        "access5150d5qulb",
        "recoverybluq5d5150",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_d5qulb)) == 6
    if target_platforms < 1 or native_builds < 0:
        return False, native_builds, "platform-matrix"
    if native_builds != target_platforms or clean_launches != target_platforms:
        return False, clean_launches, "clean-install-gap"
    if missing_tk_assets > 0:
        return False, missing_tk_assets, "tk-resource-gap"
    if artifact_digests != target_platforms:
        return False, artifact_digests, "artifact-identity"
    assert clean_launches == target_platforms
    return True, clean_launches, "platform-native-release"
